---
title: "Supervised Learning"
page-layout: full

format:
    html:
        pagetitle: "Notes | Supervised Learning"
---

::: {.hidden}
$$
\def\X{{\mathcal{X}}}
\def\Y{{\mathcal{Y}}}
\def\C{{\mathcal{C}}}
\def\H{{\mathcal{H}}}
\def\D{{\mathcal{D}}}
$$
:::

## Overview


## Notation 

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Instance Space

An *instance space* is a set $\X$ that contains the objects we may want to label; i.e., this is the domain of the function we want to learn.
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Instance

An *instance* is an element $x \in \X$. Generally, $x$ is a vector of $n$ coordinates; i.e., 
$$
x = (x_1, x_2, \ldots x_n).
$$

The coordinates of $x$ are called *attributes* or *features*.
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Hypothesis

todo
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Hypothesis Space

todo
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Concept

todo
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Definition: Concept Class

todo
:::

## Classification

todo

## Regression

todo

## Empirical Risk Minimization

todo
